metadata.official_name: State University of New York at Stony Brook
report_source_files: Stony Brook University Common Data Set 2024-2025

strategic_profile.executive_summary: Stony Brook University is a flagship public research institution in the SUNY system, recognized globally for its elite STEM, medical, and computer science programs. Situated on Long Island, it offers a highly diverse, research-intensive environment with a growing reputation that has driven application volumes to record highs. While overall acceptance rates remain around 49%, admission into its impacted programs like Computer Science is intensely competitive.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic with heavy emphasis on academic rigor, particularly in math and sciences.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Waitlist admission plummeted to 0.2% in 2024 (down from 43% the prior year), indicating extreme yield protection and over-enrollment management.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should treat a waitlist offer as a soft rejection and secure other options immediately.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Out-of-state and international acceptance rates (61.6% and 73% respectively) are significantly higher than the in-state acceptance rate (41.5%).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Stony Brook leverages non-resident enrollment to subsidize state funding deficits, offering an admissions advantage to full-pay out-of-state and international applicants.

analyst_takeaways.insight: CS majors not directly admitted are placed in an 'Area of Interest' (AOI) requiring a 3.2+ GPA across heavily rigorous filter courses (CSE 114, 214, 215).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applying undeclared to bypass CS competition is highly risky; failing to meet the strict AOI GPA cutoff prevents declaration of the major.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Heavily diverse with a large commuter population (nearly 25-48% commute). The social scene is quieter on weekends, driven more by academic intensity and specialized student organizations than traditional Greek life.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located 60 miles from NYC with an on-campus LIRR train station, allowing direct access to the city for internships and social outings, though a car is beneficial for local Long Island travel.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Co-manager of Brookhaven National Laboratory, offering undergraduates exceptionally high-level research opportunities in physics, medicine, and applied sciences.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional through at least the 2024-2025 cycle. Scores are recommended for restricted majors and honors programs.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Record high application volume. Waitlist admit rate dropped drastically from 43% to under 1%.
Massive waitlist movement this cycle, atypical for the institution.

gpa.notes: 53% of the entering class earned a high school GPA of 95 or above.

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Applied Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly competitive. Applicants must have strong calculus and physics grades, alongside high SAT/ACT math scores if submitting.
colleges.student_archetype: Intensely focused STEM student, heavy research and technical project orientation.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Grades of B- or higher required in core prerequisites. Must apply to matriculate from 'Area of Interest' (AOI) status.

core_courses: CSE 312 Social, Legal, and Ethical Issues in Computing

majors.notes: AOI students must complete requirements within one year. Transferring into CS from outside the College of Engineering is extremely difficult and relies strictly on capacity.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students seeking pre-med tracks (Biology, Chemistry) or social sciences (Psychology).
colleges.student_archetype: Pre-health track students and natural science researchers.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: Most popular major at the university with over 600 graduates annually.

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: Highly utilized as a primary pathway for pre-med students targeting the Renaissance School of Medicine.

colleges.name: College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with strong math skills aiming for finance or accounting.

majors.name: Business Management
majors.special_requirements: Requires strong performance on SAT/ACT math sections if submitting.
majors.notes: One of the top 3 most popular majors on campus.

colleges.name: School of Health Technology and Management
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students committed to clinical health professions (e.g., Nursing, Respiratory Care, Physical Therapy).
colleges.housing_profile: Commuter and clinical rotation heavy in upper division.

majors.name: Health Science
majors.notes: Serves as preparatory studies for advanced clinical placements and graduate medical programs.

application_deadlines.notes: Priority deadline for Honors College, WISE, and University Scholars.
Final deadline for restricted programs like Scholars for Medicine.

supplemental_requirements.details: 250 words: Please discuss why you are a good fit for your preferred University Honors Program.
650 words: What aspects about medicine/dental medicine intrigue you? Describe how these aspects influence your life.
Three 250-word short responses regarding desire to pursue STEM, obtaining a PhD, and diversity in STEM.
Submit 10-15 pages of your best and most characteristic writing.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Legacy is explicitly marked as 'Not Considered' to ensure fairness as part of the SUNY system. Deep STEM involvement, high math grades, and research experience heavily tip the scales.

major_selection_tactics: If targeting Computer Science, strong math grades and test scores are non-negotiable. Applying 'Area of Interest' (AOI) provides a conditional pathway, but carries immense risk as a 3.2 GPA in specific filter classes is required to declare.
Out-of-state and international applicants face considerably higher acceptance rates compared to in-state candidates, making SBU an excellent target for non-residents.
Consider Information Systems (ISE) as an alternate major to CS. It shares overlapping foundation courses but has slightly more forgiving entry requirements.
college_ranking_tactics: Leverage the Honors College application to secure early review and access to priority registration and distinct housing.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying undeclared to circumvent impacted majors is a known but risky strategy. While you can enter the university, internal transfer into capped programs like Computer Science requires near-perfect grades in prerequisite weed-out courses and is constrained by seat availability.

scholarships.name: Excelsior Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition for NYS residents making under $100k/year.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers cost of in-state tuition and fees for highly exceptional applicants.

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Additional $2,000 if SBU is listed as top choice.

scholarships.name: Honors College Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Most Honors College students receive some level of funding.

ap_policy.general_rule: SUNY system guarantees college credit for scores of 3 or higher on AP exams.
exceptions: Engineering and Computer Science programs frequently require a 4 or 5, or direct students to decline the credit in favor of taking the foundational sequences at SBU.
ap_policy.usage: Can satisfy general education requirements or act as elective credit.

ib_policy.general_rule: Scores of 4 or higher generally accepted for credit across the SUNY system.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: To fulfill CS core requirements, equivalent courses transferred must have a grade of B- or higher to declare the major from AOI.

what_it_takes: High academic rigor, specially in upper-level STEM classes.
For in-state students, standing out among thousands of highly qualified Long Island and NYC applicants.
common_activities: Research internships at labs like Brookhaven National Lab.
essay_tips: Demonstrate a clear, focused path into your major, particularly if it's STEM.
Show resilience and an ability to navigate a massive, somewhat bureaucratic state school environment.
red_flags: Low math scores on the SAT/ACT if applying to engineering or business.
Applying to an impacted major without taking related AP/IB classes in high school.
insights: SBU has a massive commuter culture; social life requires proactive effort by joining clubs or Greek life.
Students cite issues with over-enrollment and housing shortages ('temporary tripling' in dorms).
The 'AOI' (Area of Interest) status for CS is often a trap for students who underestimate the difficulty of maintaining a 3.2 GPA in the foundational 'weed-out' classes.
